questions.question: A bicycle travels $15 \text{ km}$ in $45 \text{ minutes}$. What is its speed in $\text{km/h}$?
A water tank can be filled in $4$ hours. How long will it take to fill $\frac{3}{4}$ of the tank?
If $y$ varies directly with $x$, and $y = 12$ when $x = 4$, what is the value of $y$ when $x = 6$?
A bakery sells $480$ loaves of bread per day. If they make a profit of $\$0.50$ per loaf, what is their daily profit?
A car travels from City A to City B at $60 \text{ km/h}$ and returns at $40 \text{ km/h}$. What is its average speed for the round trip?
If $y$ varies inversely with $x$, and $y = 8$ when $x = 2$, what is the value of $y$ when $x = 4$?
A group of friends want to share some candy equally. If they have $48$ pieces of candy and there are $8$ friends, how many pieces will each friend get?
A book costs $\$20$. If a $10\%$ discount is applied, how much will you pay for the book?

Tom has been saving money for a new bike and has $\$120$ saved. He wants to buy a bike that costs $\$180$. If he saves $\$10$ per week, how many weeks will it take him to have enough money to buy the bike?
A water tank can hold $3000 \text{ liters}$ of water. If $1200 \text{ liters}$ of water are already in the tank, what percentage of the tank is filled?

teacher_rules: Use a calculator only when necessary
Show all work for free-response questions
